skill.name: project-setup-architecture
skill.description: Set up React Native and Expo project infrastructure for TypeScript, SQLite, Drizzle, Zustand, testing, linting, and feature-based folders.
skill.summary: Guide React Native and Expo project setup with TypeScript, database, state, testing, and folder architecture.
skill.author: BabakBar

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many high-risk patterns, but review shows the content is a Markdown setup guide rather than executable code. The critical heuristic, weak cryptography, SQLite, reconnaissance, and documentation URL alerts are false positives, while package manager and npx setup commands are legitimate but require user awareness.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager And Project Setup Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs assistants to run npm, npx, Expo CLI, mkdir, touch, Jest, ESLint, TypeScript, and Drizzle commands. These are expected for a project setup skill, but package installation and CLI execution can download dependencies, run lifecycle scripts, and modify the project workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly documented and are relevant to the stated setup purpose. Risk comes from normal package manager behavior, not from evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives In Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and code examples, not Ruby backtick execution. They do document shell commands, but there is no hidden script, obfuscation, or command injection path in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is visibly contained in Markdown fences and checklist examples. No executable wrapper or dynamic evaluation mechanism is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Benign Documentation Links And Local Database Example
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Expo documentation, and the SQLite string is a local example database name. The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and dangerous combination alerts are not supported by the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a local SQLite filename, an app verification checklist item, and official documentation links. I found no credential access, exfiltration endpoint, weak cryptographic implementation, or obfuscated behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Installation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The guide includes npm and npx commands that may contact package registries and execute installed tooling. Users should review packages and run these commands only in trusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact package manager commands are present and are expected for the skill domain. The pattern is risky because package managers can run third-party code, not because this file shows malicious intent.

content.value_statement: New Expo projects need consistent setup for TypeScript, data, state, testing, and folder structure. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a practical checklist for configuring those foundations.
actual_capabilities: Provides TypeScript setup steps for React Native and Expo projects.
Defines a feature-based source folder structure for app code.
Shows SQLite and Drizzle setup with a database client example.
Adds Zustand state management patterns for shared app state.
Lists Jest, Testing Library, ESLint, and Prettier setup commands.
Includes verification checks for builds, tests, linting, and database access.
limitations: It is a guidance file, not an automated project generator.
Dependency versions are not pinned and may need Expo SDK compatibility checks.
Database schema design and migrations remain project-specific.
It does not replace security review of installed packages or generated files.

use_cases.title: Start A New Expo App
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to add TypeScript, folders, scripts, tests, and basic app configuration before feature work begins.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Architecture
use_cases.description: Apply shared folder conventions, state management, and verification steps across a React Native codebase.

use_cases.title: Add Data And Testing Foundations
use_cases.description: Set up SQLite, Drizzle, Jest, and React Native Testing Library for a project that needs persistence and coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize Project Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new Expo project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my Expo project and create a step-by-step setup plan for TypeScript, folders, testing, linting, and app configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Data Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to help add SQLite and Drizzle to my React Native app. Show the files to create and the verification steps to run.

prompt_templates.title: Normalize Existing Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to compare my current React Native folder structure against a feature-based architecture and suggest a migration plan.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Setup Completeness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit my Expo project setup. Check TypeScript, database, state, scripts, linting, testing, and verification gaps.

output_examples.input: Set up infrastructure for a new Expo app.
output: A sequenced setup plan for TypeScript, folders, SQLite, state, tests, linting, and scripts.
A list of commands to review before execution.
A verification checklist for compile, test, lint, database, and platform checks.

output_examples.input: Review my current React Native project structure.
output: A gap analysis against the recommended feature-based layout.
Suggested folder moves and new shared modules.
Follow-up checks to confirm imports, tests, and app startup still work.

best_practices: Review package names and versions before running install commands.
Configure one subsystem at a time and verify it before continuing.
Commit after each successful setup step so changes are easy to inspect.
anti_patterns: Do not run package manager commands in an untrusted repository without review.
Do not copy example bundle identifiers or app names into production unchanged.
Do not add database migrations without testing rollback and app startup behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete Expo app?
faq.answer: No. It guides setup tasks for an existing or newly created React Native and Expo project.

faq.question: Can I use it with Codex and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: It lists commands that an assistant may propose or run with permission. Review commands before execution.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes package manager and CLI commands. These are legitimate, but they can download dependencies and modify files.

faq.question: Does it include production database design?
faq.answer: No. It provides a local SQLite and Drizzle starting point, while schema design remains project-specific.

faq.question: What should I verify after using it?
faq.answer: Run type checks, tests, linting, app startup checks, database queries, and state management checks.
